title: JGSDF Camp Itazuma

(板妻駐屯地, *Itazuma-chūtonchi*) is a military base of the [Japan Ground Self-Defense Force](/wiki/Japan_Ground_Self-Defense_Force "Japan Ground Self-Defense Force"), located in [Gotemba](/wiki/Gotemba,_Shizuoka "Gotemba, Shizuoka"), [Shizuoka prefecture](/wiki/Shizuoka_prefecture "Shizuoka prefecture"), [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"). It is one of several military facilities located in the foothills of [Mount Fuji](/wiki/Mount_Fuji "Mount Fuji").

**JGSDF Camp Itazuma** (板妻駐屯地, *Itazuma-chūtonchi*) is a military base of the [Japan Ground Self-Defense Force](/wiki/Japan_Ground_Self-Defense_Force "Japan Ground Self-Defense Force"), located in [Gotemba](/wiki/Gotemba,_Shizuoka "Gotemba, Shizuoka"), [Shizuoka prefecture](/wiki/Shizuoka_prefecture "Shizuoka prefecture"), [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"). It is one of several military facilities located in the foothills of [Mount Fuji](/wiki/Mount_Fuji "Mount Fuji").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=JGSDF_Camp_Itazuma&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Camp Itazuma was established in April 1909 by the [Imperial Japanese Army](/wiki/Imperial_Japanese_Army "Imperial Japanese Army") as a training facility. After the end of [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), it came under the control of the [25th Infantry Division](/wiki/25th_Infantry_Division_(United_States) "25th Infantry Division (United States)") of the [United States Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army") in Japan, and was renamed “Middle Camp”.

In 1962, after the conclusion of the [Treaty of Mutual Cooperation and Security between the United States and Japan](/wiki/Treaty_of_Mutual_Cooperation_and_Security_between_the_United_States_and_Japan "Treaty of Mutual Cooperation and Security between the United States and Japan"), the base was returned to the control of the Japanese government and reestablished as a training facility for the post-war Japan Ground Self-Defense Force. It has been home to the JGSDF 34th Infantry Regiment, which has been active in various [disaster relief](/wiki/Disaster_relief "Disaster relief") operations throughout Japan since that time.
